---
title: "Quest 2: Data Types - The Shape of Data"
subtitle: "Choose the Right Rust Type for the Job"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🔷 QUEST 2 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [View Notebook](../files/lessons/02-data-types.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/02-data-types.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Rust likes clear types. This helps catch bugs early.

## 💡 Core Types

```rust
fn main() {
    let lives: i32 = 3;
    let pi: f64 = 3.14;
    let is_ready: bool = true;
    let grade: char = 'A';
    let title: &str = "Rust Rookie";

    println!("{} {} {} {} {}", lives, pi, is_ready, grade, title);
}
```

## 👨‍💻 Example

```rust
fn main() {
    let first_name = "Kai";
    let age = 14;
    let height_m = 1.62;
    let likes_rust = true;

    println!("{} is {} years old", first_name, age);
    println!("Height: {} m", height_m);
    println!("Likes Rust: {}", likes_rust);
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Create one value of each type: `i32`, `f64`, `bool`, `char`, and `&str`. Print them.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn main() {
    let score: i32 = 42;
    let temp: f64 = 21.5;
    let passed: bool = true;
    let level: char = 'B';
    let label: &str = "Beginner";

    println!("{} {} {} {} {}", score, temp, passed, level, label);
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 2 Complete
You can now read and create common Rust data types.
Next: [Quest 3: Conditionals](03-conditionals.qmd)
:::
